mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender P500 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 5.0 V8 AJ133 engine typically costs £6,000–£10,000 supply only, with fitting adding £1,600–£2,200. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Defender.

mainEntity.name: Is the Defender P500 (AJ133 V8) engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the current iteration of the AJ133 is significantly more reliable than early versions. It is a robust, characterful V8 — but it's maintenance-sensitive. Timing chain tensioner wear and coolant crossover pipe failure are the two main watchpoints.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Defender P500 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner wear (preventable with 5,000–7,500-mile oil changes), coolant crossover pipe failure, and supercharger coupler wear.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender P500 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. A well-specced P500 Defender is a £70,000–£100,000 vehicle — an £8,000 recon is only 8–11% of the car's value, making replacement a clear decision. This is well within our 60% Land Rover threshold.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Defender P500 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The P500 engine code is 508PS (AJ133) — a 5.0-litre supercharged V8. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain on a P500, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain and tensioner replacement (£2,000–£4,000) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (engine replacement at £6,000–£12,000). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Defender P500 V8 engine typically takes 16–22 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking from the front of the engine, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £2,000–£4,000 (chain and tensioner replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught before catastrophic failure)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The AJ133's timing chain tensioner wear is the single most documented issue on this engine. The good news: it's preventable. Shorten oil change intervals to 5,000–7,500 miles and use the correct 5W20 oil (STJLR.03.5004 spec). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, have the tensioners inspected immediately — catching it early saves you a £4,000+ repair bill.

problems.title: Coolant Crossover Pipe Failure
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, low coolant warning light, occasional burning smell, overheating.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant levels drop

problems.recommendation: The plastic coolant crossover pipes on the AJ133 are a known weak point — they deteriorate every 4–5 years. If you notice coolant loss or a sweet smell from the engine bay, have the crossover pipes inspected. Replacing them preventatively is far cheaper than the overheating damage they can cause if they fail completely.

problems.title: Supercharger Coupler Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling or knocking noise from the supercharger, particularly at idle, loss of supercharger efficiency.
problems.recommendation: The supercharger coupler is a wear item on the AJ133 — it's the component that connects the supercharger drive to the rotors. If you hear a rattling or marbles-in-a-tin-can noise from the supercharger area, have the coupler inspected. Replacement is straightforward and far cheaper than a complete supercharger rebuild.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender P500 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catch it before chain skips
Straightforward fix — do preventatively every 4–5 years
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Viable on lower-mileage examples; assess overall engine condition

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Defender P500? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented oil change history with 5,000–7,500-mile intervals (not 15,000-mile factory schedule)
* No cold-start rattle or supercharger noise
* Full JLR service history with correct 5W20 oil (STJLR.03.5004 spec)
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle with no timing chain history — walk away
* No record of coolant system maintenance
* Supercharger noise at idle — coupler or bearings may be failing
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — AJ133 electrical gremlins can be costly

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The AJ133 5.0 V8 is a genuinely characterful engine that transforms the Defender into a proper performance SUV. It's not fragile — but it is maintenance-sensitive. The timing chain and coolant crossover pipes are the two watchpoints; buy on documented history with evidence of shortened oil change intervals, and you'll have a robust, rewarding flagship powertrain.

cta.label: → Compare Defender P500 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender P500 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking; timing chain tensioner wear is the main concern on this engine
* Service History — including engine era (AJ133 V8) and confirmed 5,000–7,500-mile oil change intervals
* Coolant Level — Check for low coolant; crossover pipe failure is common
* Supercharger Noise — Listen for rattling or knocking at idle — coupler wear indicator
* Oil Consumption — AJ133 V8s can consume oil; monitor levels between services
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
* Fuel Economy — Expect 19–20 mpg combined; heavy consumption may indicate issues
